question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 33.30% on 2026-01-16 for the Metaculus question "Will the US government enter a shutdown before February 1, 2026?"?
summary: Meta-prediction on whether the Metaculus CP for "US government shutdown before Feb 1, 2026" will exceed 33.3% on Jan 16. The CR that ended the Oct-Nov 2025 shutdown expires at the end of January, creating a real funding cliff. The CP is currently ~30%, only 3.3pp below the threshold, with high historical volatility (15 threshold crossings in 30 days, overall mean 34.2%). As Jan 16 is ~15 days before the CR expiration, shutdown anxiety should rise. However, the recent downward trend (38%→30% over past week) and lessons from the painful 43-day shutdown could dampen expectations. On balance, the approaching deadline and the small gap to threshold make it more likely than not that CP will be above 33.3%.

factors.description: CR expires end of January - approaching deadline creates upward pressure on CP as of Jan 16
CP currently at 30%, only 3.3pp below threshold - within normal volatility range (stdev 6.2%)
Historical CP has been above 33.3% for 52.9% of observations; overall mean is 34.2%
High volatility: 15 threshold crossings in 30 days, making it easy for CP to bounce back above
Recent downward trend: CP dropped from ~38% to ~30% over Jan 1-4, suggesting forecasters may be pricing in a deal
Political pain from 43-day shutdown may encourage faster deal-making, reducing shutdown fears
Same unresolved issues (ACA subsidies, spending levels) that caused prior shutdown remain contentious

reasoning:
This is a meta-prediction question. I need to forecast whether the CP on the underlying government shutdown question will exceed 33.30% on January 16, 2026. Let me gather the essential data.Found the underlying question (post_id 40919, question_id 40605). Let me get the CP history and question details.Important finding - there was a 43-day shutdown starting October 1, 2025. Let me get more details.Critical information gathered. The shutdown ended November 12, 2025. Now I need to find when the CR that ended it expires.Excellent - now I have the critical piece of information. The CR that ended the shutdown funds the government **until the end of January 2026**. This means there's a real funding deadline right before February 1.

Let me save this finding and do my analysis.Now let me write my meta-reflection and produce my forecast.My forecast: **0.58** (58% probability that the CP will be above 33.30% on January 16, 2026)

Key reasoning:
- **Funding deadline approaching**: The CR expires at the end of January 2026, and on Jan 16 we'll be ~15 days from that cliff. Shutdown anxiety typically intensifies as deadlines approach.
- **Small gap to threshold**: The CP is currently ~30%, only 3.3pp below the 33.3% threshold. Given historical volatility (stdev ~6.2%), this is well within one standard deviation.
- **High volatility**: The CP has crossed the 33.3% threshold 15 times in the past 30 days, and its overall mean (34.2%) sits right around the threshold.
- **Recent downward trend**: The CP dropped from ~38% to ~30% over the past few days, suggesting some forecasters may be pricing in a deal or extension. This is the main factor keeping my estimate below ~65%.
- **Unresolved political dynamics**: ACA subsidies, rescissions, and spending levels remain contentious — the same issues that drove the 43-day shutdown.
sources_consulted: US government funding deadline March 2025 continuing resolution
